title: Will Pryce

[[Geoff Pryce]] (cousin)
[[Waine Pryce]] (cousin)}} }} '''Will Pryce''' (born 5 December 2002) is a professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|fb|so}} and {{rlp|sh}} for [[Hull FC]] in the [[Super League]] and the [[England Knights]] at international level. He previously played for [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the Super League and [[Newcastle Knights]] in the [[National Rugby League]]. ==Background== Pryce is of [[Jamaicans|Jamaican]] descent and is the son of former [[England national rugby league team|England]] and [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] international [[Leon Pryce]].{{Cite web|url=https://www.sthelensstar.co.uk/sport/19412650.ex-saint-leon-pryces-son-will-make-super-league-debut/|title=Ex-Saints Leon Pryce's son Will to make Super League debut|date=1 July 2021|website=St Helens Star}} His uncle [[Karl Pryce]], and distant relations [[Waine Pryce]] and Steve Pryce are former professional rugby league footballers, as was [[Geoff Pryce]].{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/9283022.waine-pryce-signs-a-deal-with-knights|title=Waine Pryce signs a deal with York City Knights|publisher=yorkpress.co.uk|date=31 December 2018|accessdate=4 July 2021}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/57470/the-rfl-pays-tribute-to-geoff-pryce|title=The RFL pays tribute to Geoff Pryce|website=www.rugby-league.com}} He played his junior rugby league for Siddal, has played for England at youth level{{Cite web|url=https://grmsport.com/blogfeed/2019/7/16/the-son-of-gb-star-leon-pryce-is-set-to-take-centre-stage-in-england-academy-test|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210709184154/https://grmsport.com/blogfeed/2019/7/16/the-son-of-gb-star-leon-pryce-is-set-to-take-centre-stage-in-england-academy-test|url-status=usurped|archive-date=9 July 2021|title=THE SON OF GB STAR LEON PRYCE IS SET TO TAKE CENTRE STAGE IN ENGLAND ACADEMY TEST!|website=GRM SPORT | GRASSROOTS MULTI-MEDIA}} and is a product of the Giants Academy system. ==Playing career== ===2021=== In 2021, he made his [[Super League]] début for [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] against the [[Catalans Dragons]].{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/match/EVP3543381|title=Super League: Huddersfield Giants v Catalans Dragons|publisher=BBC}} ===2022=== In round 6 of the 2022 Super League season, Pryce was sent off for a dangerous tackle in Huddersfield's 14-6 loss against [[Hull F.C.]] Pryce was subsequently suspended for ten matches.{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/12572912/super-league-huddersfield-giants-will-pryce-suspended-for-10-games-by-rfl-after-red-card#:~:text=against%20Hull%20FC-,Huddersfield%20Giants'%20Will%20Pryce%20has%20been%20handed%20a%2010%2Dgame,League%20defeat%20against%20Hull%20FC./|title=Super League: Huddersfield Giants' Will Pryce suspended for 10 games by RFL after red card|website=www.skysports.com}} In December, it was announced that Pryce had signed a two-year contract with the [[Newcastle Knights]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]], starting in 2024.{{Cite web|url=https://www.newcastleknights.com.au/news/2022/12/05/knights-announce-english-signings/|title=Knights announce English signings|date=December 5, 2022|website=Newcastle Knights}}{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/63858860|title=Kai Pearce-Paul & Will Pryce to join NRL side Newcastle Knights after 2023 season|website=www.bbc.co.uk}} ===2023=== Pryce played 17 matches with Huddersfield in the 2023 Super League season as the club finished ninth on the table and missed the playoffs.{{cite web|url=https://www.totalrl.com/ian-watson-explains-why-huddersfield-giants-underperformed-in-super-league-2023/#:~:text=HUDDERSFIELD%20GIANTS%20have%20been%20one,silverware%20once%20more%20this%20season./|title=Ian Watson explains why Huddersfield Giants underperformed in Super League 2023|website=www.totalrl.com}} ===2024=== In round 17 of the [[2024 NRL season]], Pryce made his [[National Rugby League|NRL]] debut, scoring a try in Newcastle's 34-26 win over the [[Parramatta Eels]].{{Cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2024/06/29/will-pryce-scores-in-nrl-debut-as-eels-lead-knights/|title=Pryce is right as Best powers Knights home against Eels|date=29 June 2024|website=National Rugby League}} Pryce was limited to just six games for Newcastle in the [[2024 NRL season]] as the club finished 8th and qualified for the finals.{{cite web|url=https://www.nine.com.au/sport/nrl/news-2024-the-mole-exclusive-newcastle-knights-season-review-adam-obrien-20240917-p5kb9j.html/|title=Brutal reality Newcastle must accept before solving confidence-killing 'riddle'|website=www.nine.com.au}} === 2025 === On 31 March 2025, it was announced that Pryce had joined [[Hull FC]] in the [[Super League]], on a 3½-year deal, after being allowed to leave [[Newcastle Knights]] on [[compassionate grounds]], to return to the UK.{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=31 Mar 2025|author=Ben Olawumi|title=Hull FC confirm Will Pryce signing as length of long-term contract revealed|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/hull-fc-confirm-will-pryce-signing-as-length-of-long-term-contract-revealed}}
